description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three angular, faceted stones of varying sizes, each with a low-poly geometric appearance. The stones are primarily shades of gray, with subtle variations in tone that suggest different mineral compositions or lighting effects. The largest stone is tall and sharply pointed, while the other two are more squat and irregular, creating a visually interesting arrangement. The surfaces are matte and lack any visible veining, carvings, or significant weathering, giving the stones a freshly quarried or minimally aged look. The edges and faces are sharply defined, with no rounded or eroded features, indicating limited exposure to natural elements. This type of stone grouping is commonly used as a decorative or functional barrier in urban parks, plazas, or landscaped sidewalks.
description: A cluster of three angular, faceted gray stones with sharp edges and a matte finish, arranged in a visually striking group. The stones vary in size and shape, with the largest being tall and pointed.
description_view_0: Three distinct stones are visible, with the tallest stone in the center, flanked by a medium and a smaller stone, all showing sharp, angular faces.
description_view_1: The tallest stone dominates the view, with the medium stone to the left and the smallest stone partially obscured behind.
description_view_2: The rear of the cluster is visible, showing the broad, flat back of the tallest stone and the side of the medium stone.
description_view_3: The arrangement is seen from the opposite side, highlighting the medium stone in the foreground and the tallest stone behind.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with similar stones on the ground
attribute_pattern: No visible pattern; surfaces are uniformly colored with faceted, geometric planes and no veining or carvings.
attribute_weathering: Surfaces appear smooth and freshly cut, with no significant signs of erosion, lichen, or cracking.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically placed to contrast with paved surfaces or planted areas, serving as a visual and physical barrier or decorative element.
